target.name: Mendra
target.founder: Joshua Grass, Jeff Ajer, Lalarukh Haris Shaikh

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $82M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 5.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

private.name: Zeta Surgical
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series A), post-money $100M

data.narrative:
Mendra is a Series A-stage biotech and health company that has raised $82M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms in biotech and health at similar or adjacent stages, including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $82M raised as a proxy for current value, implying $164M. Peer-median valuation anchors to 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $100M to $3.3B and a median of $805M, directly implying $805M. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of disclosed revenue for the target. 

Triangulating the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $139M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $484M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around technology maturity, near-term revenue potential, and competitive positioning within the frontier biotech sector. The gap between stage-progression ($164M) and peer-median ($805M) suggests either that Mendra's peers command significant premiums for later-stage validation or that the peer set skews toward higher-outcome companies. Revenue clarity and clinical/commercial milestones would narrow this range materially.
